programmes.show_description: Recorded live at Hammersmith Apollo, comedian Bill Bailey is furious and hilarious as he takes on popular culture, social media, celebrity, cheap shark-diving and more.
(2021) Paul Verhoeven's historical drama. Sex and the sacred collide as a Renaissance nun begins to have visions of Christ, while also taking a shine to one of her sisters. In French/subs.
Jessica Fostekew and Joe Lycett enjoy a Canarian caper, as they trike through Lanzarote's lunar landscape, sample volcanic vino and cactus croquettes, and tour the lava tunnels.
Natalie Palmer flies solo as she hosts at The Oak pub and B&B in Charing, Kent. Her guests admire her work ethic, but they're not blown away by the extractor fans.
Dennis Gilligan hosts at The White Lady in Worstead, Norfolk. Will his rivals encounter the legendary ghostly guest?
Former couple Katherine and Stewart offer an alternative spiritual experience at Cranleigh House B&B in Devon. But Dennis and Gary struggle to keep a straight face during a hilltop healing.
The last visit is to Paignton in Devon, where Mike and Denise are the new kids on the block, having only opened the doors to their B&B, Beaches, a few weeks earlier.
It's the final day of the competition and tensions run high as the B&Bers meet for the final time to air their grievances, before the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie's Fill Your House for Free
programmes.show_description: Kirstie Allsopp and the team come to the rescue of Dave and Kate, whose house has been flooded.
Lyin' Hearted: Carrie discovers a secret about Arthur. Doug regrets befriending a quirky stranger.
Life Sentence: When Doug installs a camera in Arthur's room, his friends get addicted to Arthur TV.
Veiled Threat: Carrie and Doug reminisce about their wedding.
Party Dress: Ray and Debra disagree over whether they should buy an expensive dress for Ally to wear to a friend's formal 13th birthday party.
Security: After Robert takes a second job selling security systems, he reveals that he's been asked to join the firm and is mulling over retiring from the police force.
The Ingrate: Debra's hurt when Ray receives an honorary doctorate and thanks everyone except her in his acceptance speech.
The Late Doctor Crane: Reports of Frasier's death are greatly exaggerated. Nonetheless, he's disappointed to realise that he hasn't achieved what he'd set out to do in life.
The Apparent Trap: When Lilith drops Frederick off at Frasier's house for Thanksgiving, Frederick asks her to stay for dinner. Soon, strange things start to happen...
Back Talk: When Frasier's back goes, his prescribed medication causes him to lose his faculties and tell Daphne a long-held secret.
Lisa the Beauty Queen: Lisa wins second place in a beauty contest.
Itchy and Scratchy: The Movie: While Homer and Marge are at parents' evening, Bart breaks the babysitter's teeth. So Homer vows never to let him see the Itchy and Scratchy film.
...Highlights: Verstappen has taken pole in the last four Japanese GPs. But with Mercedes appearing dominant this season, the master of Suzuka has got his work cut out to do it again.
Marge Gets a Job: When Marge gets a job at the power plant, Mr Burns falls in love with her and invites her over to dinner, complete with a kidnapped Tom Jones to serenade them.
New Kid on the Block: When Homer gets kicked out of an all-you-can-eat seafood restaurant for eating too much, he takes the owner to court.
The week begins at the Peniarth Arms in Wales, with hosts Susanne and Anwen. Toby and Kath feel like they've been transported in time, while Ryan and Sarah kick up a fuss about their room.
The second destination of the week is Apple Camping in Pembrokeshire. Host Toby's guests appreciate his unique take on glamping, but a lack of basics makes for some not-so-happy campers.
At The Garden House in Somerset, host and chef Ryan hopes that his brekkie will impress his guests. But some find the food difficult to stomach, resulting in some hard-to-swallow feedback.
James hosts the week's final visit at Ivy House in St Austell. His guests marvel at the delightful views, but the lack of a lock and a substandard sausage put James under scrutiny.
The B&Bers meet on payment day to settle some scores. Sleep troubles and breakfast bugbears are on the agenda, before the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
(2019) Fact-based action adventure with Willem Dafoe and a cheeky Siberian husky pup who becomes one hell of a pack leader and potential life saver during the harsh Alaskan winter of 1925.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
...Journeys: All aboard for some luxury train rides across Britain. This episode rolls along the stunning Devon coastline, with comfy carriages and excellent fine dining. (Ep1/4)
Witnesses, survivors and front-line heroes recall the first days of the Chernobyl disaster of April 1986. Featuring rare archive footage of the world's worst ever nuclear accident.
On Sa Pa's stunning hills, Bill explores longstanding cultural traditions - plus mountain-top ice cream, the friendship of a buffalo and the healing properties of a tiny barrel bath. (Ep5)
(2022) Sci-fi disaster with Halle Berry. A NASA bigwig, a disgraced astronaut and a conspiracy theorist are all that stand between the Earth and a plummeting Moon. But are aliens involved?
(2022) Don't-go-down-into-the-cellar horror with Georgina Campbell and Bill Skarsgård. A woman enters some very dark places below the double-booked Airbnb that she's staying in.
Landoll's Mohican Castle: Gordon Ramsay visits a custom-built, multi-million-dollar hotel in Loudonville, Ohio. Can he help the struggling business and its overworked owner?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien restore a metal workbench, a pine chest and the seat from an old pony and trap.
Lisa the Beauty Queen: Lisa wins second place in a beauty contest.
Itchy and Scratchy: The Movie: While Homer and Marge are at parents' evening, Bart breaks the babysitter's teeth. So Homer vows never to let him see the Itchy and Scratchy film.
Marge Gets a Job: When Marge gets a job at the power plant, Mr Burns falls in love with her and invites her over to dinner, complete with a kidnapped Tom Jones to serenade them.
Oxy Moron: Carrie's upset when, on a bumpy plane landing, Doug takes the only oxygen mask. Things don't go as well as hoped for Spence with the hot Brazilian exchange student.
Depo Man: Carrie's embarrassed when Doug is called as an expert witness for a case she's working on. Meanwhile, Danny and Spence fight over Holly the dog walker.
Ovary Action: Doug's parents' visit coincides with Carrie's 'fertile time'.
Crazy Chin: Amy's parents put Robert on the defensive when they point out his strange habit of touching food to his chin before he eats it.
The Nice Talk: When the MacDougalls are invited to spend Easter with the Barones, the families attempt to bond over a 500-piece jigsaw puzzle.
The Fight Before Christmas: An apprehensive Daphne believes that Niles is finally going to reveal his true feelings for her. Frasier throws a poorly attended party.
RDWRER: Frasier and Niles are forced to spend their Millennium New Year driving to Sun Valley in Martin's Winnebago.
Mr Plow: Homer impulsively buys a snow plough and sets up in business as Mr Plow. But when Barney declares himself the Plow King, their rivalry spirals out of control.
Lisa's First Word: The family try to get Maggie to say her first word. But Homer's glad that she hasn't spoken yet, because all Lisa and Bart do is talk back to him.
Les Dennis chats about the theatre tour of the musical Waitress. Plus: Richard Kind, Fatiha El-Ghorri, Keith Brymer Jones, Marj Hogarth, Courtney Pine and The Pale White.
The Mercedes drivers have a win apiece as they head to Japan, but Verstappen loves it here at Suzuka. Can he throw a spanner in the works of the Silver Arrows' Russell and Antonelli?
Golf fan Peggy can't think of anywhere else she'd rather buy a holiday home than on Spain's Costa del Sol. Danni Menzies tees up five properties for £115k, but will any score a hole-in-one?
A couple create an ambitious floating home, George and Will make a magical vista in their garden flat, and two siblings build their own mobile homes away from home.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
(2000) Tom Hanks and Tim Allen return in toy form for more endearing animated adventures. Buzz rallies his troops to foil a dastardly collector's plan to put Woody in a box.
...Off for Stand Up To Cancer: Molly-Mae Hague, Richard Herring, JoJo Siwa and Babatunde Aléshé make decorated traybakes and tarte tatin, and recreate childhood toys in biscuits.

programmes.show_name: New: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programmes.show_description: The epic adventure reality contest continues. The Predators ruthlessly round on one player and cracks form in a friendship. As politics and power plays kick in, who'll be culled next? (Ep4)
The Goggleboxers settle in for Celebrity Bake Off, The Dog House, Saturday Night Live UK, Britain's Got Talent, Celebrity Sabotage, BBC News on Easter eggs and more.
A musical waiter, a Boris-loving singleton and an '80s-obsessed romantic - this time First Dates serves up chaos over a spilled drink, a bit of chemistry and a few curveballs.
